title: Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral
tagline: Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral is a much-loved heritage city in Kerala, known for forts and architecture.
overview.description: Arthat St. Mary's Orthodox Cathedral (Arthat Valiyapally), also called Kunnamkulam-Chattukulangara Church, is an ancient Oriental Orthodox church located in Arthat village of Thalapilly taluk, one mile south of Kunnamkulam town, in Thrissur district, Kerala, India. It is one of the oldest Christian churches in the region. This church did not participate in the Synod of Diamper.

topPlaces.name: Thaikkad
topPlaces.description: Thaikkad is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kanipayyur
topPlaces.description: Kanippayyur is a village, near Kunnamkulam in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. Kanippayyur is prominent in the practice of Traditional Architecture, Temple Building, Astrology and related studies.

topPlaces.name: Iringaprom
topPlaces.description: Iringaprom is a census town in Thrissur district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Punnathurkotta
topPlaces.description: Punnathurkotta is a fort and former palace located in Kottapadi, about 3 km from the Guruvayoor Sree Krishna Temple, in Thrissur District of Kerala State in South India.

topPlaces.name: Kandanissery
topPlaces.description: Kandanissery is a small village in the Indian state of Kerala. Guruvayur, a well known Hindu pilgrimage centre, is very near to Kandanissery.

topPlaces.name: Kottapadi
topPlaces.description: Kottapadi is a village near Guruvayur town in the district of Thrissur, Kerala, South India. Kottapadi literally translates to Gates to Fort.

topPlaces.name: Kunnamkulam
topPlaces.description: Kunnamkulam Malayalam: [kun̪ːəm kuɭ̺ːəm] is a municipal town located in the Thrissur District of Kerala, India, with historical origins tracing back to the Paleolithic age. Its strategic location and excellent connectivity make it an important economic and cultural node, linking the southern regions of Kerala with North Malabar, and serving as a crucial point for travel and trade.

topPlaces.name: Cheruvathur Mahadeva Temple
topPlaces.description: Cheruvathur Sri Mahadeva Temple, located in Kunnamkulam town of Thrissur district of Kerala. The presiding deity of the temple is Shiva, located in main sanctum sanatorium facing east.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral?
faq.a: The best time to visit Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (77 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral?
faq.a: Stays in Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Arthat St. Mary's Cathedral?
faq.a: The top attractions are Thaikkad, Kanipayyur, Iringaprom, Punnathurkotta, Kandanissery.
